steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Mrs. Sommers experiences a shift in her sense of self as a direct result of putting on clothing. The correct quotation must show the clothing causing an internal change — not just physical admiration of clothes, pre-transformation mindset, or thoughts about others.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Mrs. Sommers was one who knew the value of bargains; who could stand for hours making her way inch by inch toward the desired object that was selling below cost.": Describes her pre-transformation character as a frugal shopper. No clothing, no shift in self. ✗
  • Choice B — "She held back her skirts and turned her feet one way and her head another way as she glanced down at the polished, pointed-tipped boots.": Depicts physical admiration of boots, but no internal change is stated or implied. ✗
  • Choice C — "She would buy so and so many yards of percale for new shirt waists for the boys and Janie and Mag.": Shows her thinking about her family's needs — no clothing worn, no self-perception shift. ✗
  • Choice D — "Her stockings and boots and well fitting gloves had worked marvels in her bearing—had given her a feeling of assurance, a sense of belonging to the well-dressed multitude.": The clothing (stockings, boots, gloves) directly causes two internal changes: assurance and a sense of belonging. This is the causal link the claim describes. ✓
That matches Choice D — "Her stockings and boots and well fitting gloves had worked marvels in her bearing—had given her a feeling of assurance, a sense of belonging to the well-dressed multitude.".
